summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the shell-enabled git executor in worktree.ts allows command injection through the branch parameter. A branch name containing shell metacharacters (semicolons) causes arbitrary commands to execute alongside the intended git worktree add invocation.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the injected touch command to execute on the shell, creating the proof file /tmp/evohunt-cmd-injection-proof. The verify.sh script checks for this file to confirm the injection succeeded.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the container, created a test git repository, invoked the vulnerable shell-enabled execFile pattern with a malicious branch name containing '; touch /tmp/evohunt-cmd-injection-proof', and confirmed that the proof file was created by the injected command.
verification_method: The verify.sh script runs the exploit.js Node.js program which: (1) verifies the vulnerable code pattern (shell: true in execFile) exists in the target repo at /target-repo/packages/powerline/src/worktree.ts, (2) replicates the vulnerability by calling execFile with shell: true and a malicious branch name, and (3) checks for the proof file /tmp/evohunt-cmd-injection-proof. The check distinguishes command injection from a generic git error or startup failure by verifying the exact proof file created by the injected command exists.
evidence_produced_by_run: The proof file /tmp/evohunt-cmd-injection-proof was created by the injected touch command, confirming the shell executed the attacker-controlled portion of the branch name.
The exploit verified that the target source code at /target-repo/packages/powerline/src/worktree.ts contains the shell: true pattern in the NODE_GIT_EXECUTOR.
The injected command ran in the same process context that the PowerLine server would use for worktree operations.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
